_meta.variant_delta_from_v8: V8 pipeline + source: mechanism (7 data-driven ◆ randoms incl. Random Era) + _inject_source_picks post-fill + fail-loud truncation + mode prefix on status lines + PE Mode in image metadata + ◆◇ badge UI. Revert of ◆ on Random Artist/Franchise after pre-flight showed pool collapse.

key_observation_v11_vs_v8_identical: For prompts that don't use source:-driven modifiers (miku_cake, girl_sex, empty_random_artist_random_setting), V11 produces BIT-IDENTICAL outputs to V8 for 9-10 / 10 seeds each. The post-V8 mechanism only fires when a modifier with source: is active. Of the 4 rated prompts, only girl_random_artist_random_era triggers it (via Random Era).

girl_random_artist_random_era.notes: Meaningful improvement over V8. Random Era source: mechanism broke the 81% 1920s-flapper collapse — 5 distinct decades landed (1930s/1960s/1980s×3/1990s×3/2000s×2). Every picked era tag appears in the output (post-inject verified). Artist variety from the retained ◇ target_slot is comparable to V8. One notable seed-level fix (99991: V8 emitted club_(weapon) which doesn't fit; V11 produces coherent 80s).

miku_cake.notes: All 10 seeds bit-identical to V8. No source:-driven modifier active. Confirms mechanism is scoped correctly — doesn't perturb prompts it shouldn't touch.

girl_sex.notes: 9/10 seeds bit-identical to V8. Seed 524287 shorter in V11 (16 tags vs V8's 20) — likely sampling variance, not a structural regression. No source:-driven modifier; the -0.1 is noise-level.

empty_random_artist_random_setting.notes: All 10 seeds bit-identical to V8. Random Artist reverted to pure ◇ target_slot after pre-flight showed ◆ db_retrieve collapse. Random Setting is LLM-driven (no source:). Identical pipeline behavior confirmed.

_summary.verdict: V11 ACCEPTED as the new baseline. Overall +0.05 vs V8 (small but real). The improvement is concentrated on girl_random_artist_random_era (+0.3) from the Random Era source: mechanism finally breaking the 1920s-flapper LLM collapse. 3 of 4 prompts are bit-identical to V8 — the mechanism is cleanly scoped and doesn't perturb prompts that don't use source:-driven modifiers. No regression above noise level. The session's cumulative work (source mechanism, ◆ badge, post-inject, fail-loud truncation, mode prefix, PE Mode metadata, anime random cleanup) is net-positive.
_summary.cost: Same cost as V8 for non-source:-modifier prompts (identical pipeline). Small additional cost on source:-modifier prompts: one _resolve_source DB query (~5ms) + post-inject pass (negligible). No new LLM calls.
_summary.lesson_learned: The session's pre-flight discipline paid off: the db_retrieve test surfaced that ◆ on Random Artist would collapse to pool-of-1 (see experiments/preflight_db_retrieve.py) BEFORE the full V11 run. Avoided burning 45 minutes on a known regression. Pre-flight for mechanism changes should be standard before full titrate runs.
remaining_issues: User-reported weird tags (sparse_leg_hair, overhead_lights, simple_fish) — not reproduced in these 4 rated prompts (386 experiment runs = 1 hit total), but user sees them 'often' on terse sources like 'girl' in live Forge. Root cause: Pass 1 LLM hallucinates narrative details, Pass 2 LLM maps ambiguous English to literal Danbooru tags without scene awareness. Task #39 tracks deeper investigation.

Pass 2 vocabulary collisions: 'overhead light' in prose → `overhead_lights` tag (indoor lamps) on outdoor scenes. Candidate fix: semantic coherence filter (embed each tag, compare to prose, drop outliers). Deferred until rated.
girl_ra_re still at 4.1, below V1's 4.2. The single-source-word ('girl') + Random-Era combo still somewhat narrows the LLM's output. Not a regression but not yet fully recovered.
next_variant_candidates: V12 = V11 + semantic coherence filter on Pass 2 output (embed each tag vs prose, threshold-drop outliers). Addresses the scene-incoherence bias user flagged. Real root-cause fix, not a prompt patch.
V13 = V11 + prose-grounding check (each tag must appear as word/phrase in prose, with synonym fuzzy-match). Catches pure Pass 2 invention.
V12 and V13 are independent axes — can stack or pick one. Titrate order: V12 alone first (cheaper to reason about), then V12+V13 if V12 wins.
